title: Zhenan Bao Interview
text:
hi Janan it's good to see you again hi
we first met what 20 years ago something
like that although we both look
incredibly young uh it was that that
long ago uh fantastic talk today uh the
breadth of your work is truly impressive
uh as as you've highlighted your
motivation is to solve problems that uh
will help humans in the future and maybe
enhance how humans can exist you want to
talk a little bit about that Vision you
have mhm yeah our um long-term vision is
uh to to mimic human skin uh and uh in
terms of building a electronic sheet
that uh mimics the sensing functionality
of human skin and uh looks just like
human skin and feels like human skin uh
but since we're building this artificial
um uh sheet or electronic sheet then uh
it gives us the possibility to to add
additional functionalities that our
human scheme may not have uh so that
those could be um sensing the chemical
environment around us or uh sensing uh
the um uh the heat from far away or or
rather than close up right instead of
being in contact or maybe even read uh
people's thoughts
without uh that's a good one you know
what it reminds I know just
did you ever watch the $6 million man
when you were a kid do you remember the
show the $6 Million Man and there was a
$6 million woman but he had a bionic arm
and a bionic and bionic ey uh and and
the idea was that he could perceive
beyond what was humanly possible uh and
then could do amazing things who would
jump higher and he could hear at
distance Etc I think do you think that
world will exist I've always thought
science fiction well when well thought
out does in fact reflect a reasonable
future reality and it sounds like you're
describing that real
yeah well the the way I I think about it
is that uh those type of scenario that
we see in science fiction uh they some
form of it eventually become uh realized
in our everyday life may not be exactly
the same form uh but eventually uh they
Inspire us to to think about developing
technology that can uh can have the same
function but then we find better usage
rather than of superhero type things but
I I agree with you that when in Bell
labs when we look at problems now uh we
we look for what is a future human need
and one of the ways you can find that is
actually in science F fiction literature
because a version of that does represent
what humans want otherwise it wouldn't
be uh wouldn't resonate with people so
let's talk a little bit about Bell Labs
uh you're back uh welcome back thank you
what did you learn from B ABS that
you've taken with you on your journey uh
and uh do you feel that Bell Labs is
still relevant today maybe those are the
two questions yeah there there were so
many things that that I learned from B
Labs that really shaped how how I think
about problem uh and how I approach
problem uh the to to make long story
short uh I think it's about the um
collaboration between people uh from
different disciplines things uh the
diversity uh of uh people coming from
different background and gender uh and
also the um uh the the general uh
environment of nurturing environment uh
um for uh younger scientists to um to
Mentor them to to help them to become
successful uh and and all of those were
really things that uh greatly impact how
how I um Assemble my current research
group and how I uh approach problems
it's really uh exciting to be back and
also really exciting to see all the new
things that are going on uh in Bell labs
and the new
directions uh that will um uh on uh the
um human machine interface and um uh uh
enhancing human uh functionality we've
end up in a similar Place haven't we
going after the idea of enhancing human
functionality I think your group is
focused on the sensor part us the
networking of Those sensors so I see us
coming back together yes which is
perfect so you came at the right time
welcome back again well thanks for
having me and well done on all your
successes we're very proud of you thank
you thanks very much
